title: centaurea × adeana
intro: Centaurea × adeana, also known as the Ammi Major or false bishop's weed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Centaurea × adeana include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and alleviating symptoms of allergies and colds. Centaurea × adeana is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and can also be used in herbal remedies, such as teas, salves, and ointments.
